Feature_Name: L1/L2 Triggered Mobility (LTM) Handover UE Context Modification Procedure

3GPP_Reference.Title: NG-RAN; F1 Application Protocol (F1AP), NG-RAN; Architecture description

Feature_Description: The UE Context Modification procedure is used to modify the established UE Context, including establishing, modifying, and releasing radio resources or sidelink resources. This procedure is also used to command the gNB-DU to stop data transmission for the UE for mobility. The procedure uses UE-associated signalling.

For L1/L2 Triggered Mobility (LTM), this procedure is used when the UE moves within the same gNB-DU (Intra-gNB-DU LTM) or from one gNB-DU to another gNB-DU within the same gNB-CU (Inter-gNB-DU LTM) during NR operation. It involves the gNB-CU sending a UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message to the gNB-DU, and the gNB-DU responding with a UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE message, to configure and manage LTM specific parameters and resources.
Feature_Objectives: To modify the established UE Context for L1/L2 Triggered Mobility (LTM) handover procedures.
To establish, modify, and release radio resources or sidelink resources as required for LTM.
To command the gNB-DU to stop data transmission for the UE during LTM mobility.
To configure LTM specific parameters such as CSI resource configuration, LTM configuration ID mapping, early synchronization information, CFRA resources, and L2 reset information.
To enable the gNB-DU to provide generated LTM configurations and early UL synchronization information to the gNB-CU.
Feature_Implementation_Steps: The gNB-CU initiates the UE Context Modification procedure by sending a UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message to the gNB-DU.
Upon reception of the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message, the gNB-DU shall perform the requested modifications.

If the LTM Information Modify IE is included in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message and its LTM Indicator IE is set to 'true', the gNB-DU shall consider that the request concerns LTM for the included SpCell ID IE.
The gNB-DU shall regard this as a reconfiguration with sync as defined in TS 38.331 [8].

If the Request for Lower Layer Configuration IE (within Reference Configuration IE in LTM Information Modify IE) is set to 'true', the gNB-DU shall provide the lower layer configuration in the Reference Configuration Information IE (within LTM Configuration IE) in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE message for the gNB-CU to generate the LTM reference configuration.

If the Reference Configuration Information IE (within Reference Configuration IE in LTM Information Modify IE) is contained, the gNB-DU shall take it into account for generating the LTM lower layer configuration.

If the CSI Resource Configuration IE is contained in the LTM Information Modify IE, the gNB-DU shall use it to generate the LTM CSI reporting configuration in the CellGroupConfig IE for the requested LTM candidate cell.
If the LTM Configuration ID Mapping List IE is contained in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message, the gNB-DU shall consider this as the mapping information for the LTM candidate cell(s).

If the Early Sync Information Request IE is included, the gNB-DU shall include the Early Sync Information IE of the accepted candidate cell for early TA acquisition (early UL synchronisation) in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE message.

If the Early Sync Candidate Cell Information List IE or Early Sync Serving Cell Information IE is contained, the gNB-DU shall use it for early TA acquisition and UE based TA measurement during LTM cell switch.
If the LTM CFRA Resource Config List IE is contained, the gNB-DU shall use it for the LTM cell switch command.
If the LTM Cells to be Released List IE is included, the gNB-DU shall release the configured candidate cells in the list.
If the LTM Reset Information IE is contained, the gNB-DU shall take it into account for L2 reset (RLC re-establishment) during an intra-DU LTM cell switch.

If the gNB-DU accepts the request for LTM configuration, it responds with a UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE message, including the generated lower layer RRC configurations for the accepted candidate cell.
The UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE message shall include the Requested Target Cell ID IE.
If the LTM Configuration IE is included in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE message, the gNB-CU shall consider it as the generated configuration for LTM from the accepted candidate cell.

If the Complete Candidate Configuration Indicator IE (within LTM Configuration IE) is set to 'complete', the gNB-CU shall consider that the LTM candidate configuration is a complete candidate configuration.
Procedure_Implementation_Steps: Successful Operation (TS 38.473, Section 8.3.4.2):
1. The UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message is initiated by the gNB-CU.
2. Upon reception of the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message, the gNB-DU shall perform the modifications.

3. If the SpCell ID IE is included in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message and the LTM Information Modify IE is present, the gNB-DU shall consider that the request concerns LTM for the included SpCell ID IE and shall include it as the Requested Target Cell ID IE in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE message. The gNB-DU shall regard it as a reconfiguration with sync as defined in TS 38.331 [8].

4. If the Request for Lower Layer Configuration IE set to 'true' is contained within the Reference Configuration IE in the LTM Information Modify IE included in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message, the gNB-DU shall, if supported, include the Reference Configuration Information IE in the LTM Configuration IE in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE message to provide lower layer configuration for the gNB-CU to generate the LTM reference configuration.

5. If the Reference Configuration Information IE is contained within the Reference Configuration IE in the LTM Information Modify IE included in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message, the gNB-DU shall, if supported, take it into account for generating the LTM lower layer configuration.

6. If the CSI Resource Configuration IE is contained in the LTM Information Modify IE included in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message and the SpCell ID IE is also included, the gNB-DU shall, if supported, use it to generate the LTM CSI reporting configuration in the CellGroupConfig IE for the requested LTM candidate cell identified by the SpCell ID IE.

7. If the CSI Resource Configuration IE is contained in the LTM Information Modify IE included in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message while the SpCell ID IE is absent, the gNB-DU shall, if supported, use it to generate the LTM CSI reporting configuration in the CellGroupConfig IE for the serving cell.

8. If the LTM Configuration ID Mapping List IE is contained in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message, the gNB-DU shall, if supported, consider this as the mapping information for the LTM candidate cell(s).

9. If the Early Sync Information Request IE is included in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message, the gNB-DU shall, if supported, include Early Sync Information IE of the accepted candidate cell for early TA acquisition (early UL synchronisation) in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE message.

10. If the Early Sync Candidate Cell Information List IE is contained in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message, the gNB-DU shall, if supported, use it as specified in TS 38.401 [4]. If the UE Based TA Measurement Configuration IE is contained in the Early Sync Candidate Cell Information List IE for some candidate cell, the gNB-DU shall, if supported, take them into account for UE based TA measurement during LTM cell switch as specified in TS 38.331 [8].

11. If the Early Sync Serving Cell Information IE is contained in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message, the gNB-DU shall, if supported, use it as specified in TS 38.401 [4]. If the UE Based TA Measurement Configuration IE is contained in the Early Sync Serving Cell Information IE, the gNB-DU shall, if supported, take it into account for UE based TA measurement during LTM cell switch as specified in TS 38.331 [8].

12. If the LTM CFRA Resource Config List IE is contained in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message, the gNB-DU shall, if supported, use it for the LTM cell switch command as specified in TS 38.321 [16].
13. If the LTM Cells to be Released List IE is included in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message, the gNB-DU shall, if supported, release the configured candidate cells in the list.

14. If the LTM Reset Information IE is contained in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message, the gNB-DU shall, if supported, take them into account for L2 reset (i.e., RLC re-establishment) during an intra-DU LTM cell switch as specified in TS38.331 [8].

15. If the LTM Configuration IE is included in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE message, the gNB-CU shall, if supported, consider it as the generated configuration for LTM from the accepted candidate cell in the gNB-DU.

16. If the Complete Candidate Configuration Indicator IE set to 'complete' is contained in the LTM Configuration IE included in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE message, the gNB-CU shall, if supported, consider that the LTM candidate configuration is a complete candidate configuration.
17. If successful, the gNB-DU reports the update in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE message.
Unsuccessful Operation (TS 38.473, Section 8.3.4.3):

1. In case none of the requested modifications of the UE context can be successfully performed, the gNB-DU shall respond with the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION FAILURE message with an appropriate cause value.

2. If the LTM Information Modify IE was included, but the SpCell ID IE and the CSI Resource Configuration IE were not included in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message, the gNB-DU shall respond with the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION FAILURE message with an appropriate cause value.

3. If the gNB-DU is not able to accept the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message for mobility because an LTM command has been triggered to the UE, it shall reply with the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION FAILURE message with an appropriate cause value.

LTMInformation-Modify ::= SEQUENCE {
 lTMIndicator      LTMIndicator,
 referenceConfiguration    ReferenceConfiguration         OPTIONAL,
 cSIResourceConfiguration    CSIResourceConfiguration        OPTIONAL,
 iE-Extensions  ProtocolExtensionContainer { { LTMInformation-Modify-ExtIEs} } OPTIONAL,
 ...
}
LTMIndicator ::= ENUMERATED {true, ...}

ReferenceConfigurationInformation ::= OCTET STRING

LTM CFRA Resource Config List IE

Information_Elements.IE_Name: LTM Cells to be Released List IE

Information_Elements.IE_Definition:
LTMConfiguration ::= SEQUENCE {
 sSBInformation     SSBInformation,
 referenceConfigurationInformation  ReferenceConfigurationInformation   OPTIONAL,
 completeCandidateConfigurationIndicator  CompleteCandidateConfigurationIndicator     OPTIONAL,
 lTMCFRAResourceConfig    LTMCFRAResourceConfig      OPTIONAL,
 lTMCFRAResourceConfigSUL   LTMCFRAResourceConfig      OPTIONAL,
 iE-Extensions      ProtocolExtensionContainer { { LTMConfiguration-ExtIEs } } OPTIONAL,
 ...
}
CompleteCandidateConfigurationIndicator ::= ENUMERATED {complete, ...}
FullConfiguration ::= ENUMERATED {full, ...}

Information_Elements.IE_Name: Early UL Sync Configuration for SUL IE

Information_Elements.IE_Definition: CellGroupConfig ::= OCTET STRING

Piggy_Backed_IES_Info: The RRCReconfiguration message (RRC IE) can be piggy-backed within the RRC-Container IE of the F1AP UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message. The RRCReconfigurationComplete message (RRC IE) is sent by the UE in response to the RRCReconfiguration message. The CellGroupConfig IE (RRC IE) is transparently signaled to the UE as part of RRC Reconfiguration.

Piggy_Backed_IES_Mapping.Source_IE_Name: CU to DU RRC Information IE
DU to CU RRC Information IE
